hero_subtitle: SFTY helps VASPs connect adverse media checks on ultimate beneficial owners to rules, cases and audit evidence within a unified AML compliance operating layer.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Adverse media checks remain disconnected and slow
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to connect UBO adverse media findings with broader AML cases, creating incomplete risk views and delayed reviews. This fragmentation weakens audit readiness and complicates traceability across regulatory and operational workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects UBO adverse media results with customer profiles, alerts, case workflows and decision evidence so compliance teams can conduct faster reviews with clearer risk context and stronger audit trails.
workflow_title: From UBO adverse media alerts to cases
workflow_description: SFTY processes adverse media alerts on UBOs through risk scoring, case creation, reviewer workflow, decision logging and audit-ready compliance evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides VASPs a provider-neutral layer that turns adverse media checks into structured reviews, cases, decisions, and audit-ready evidence for effective UBO risk management.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_title: Built for UBO adverse media review
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP compliance teams with controlled provider orchestration, audit-ready evidence preservation, clear bank due diligence and enterprise-grade deployment standards.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support UBO adverse media screening workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how adverse media alerts trigger case reviews
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports UBO adverse media cases
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Compare deployment options for adverse media compliance controls
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASP compliance teams a connected platform to connect screening, monitoring, case management, regulatory rule updates and audit evidence across digital asset AML operations.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle UBO adverse media cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures adverse media alerts on UBOs into cases, links evidence, applies rules, routes for review, logs decisions and monitors regulatory changes related to adverse media risk.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and all related evidence in structured records to support review readiness and regulator audit requirements.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing KYC, KYB, sanctions and screening providers, connecting their outputs into a single operational compliance workflow.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace adverse media screening tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace screening providers but integrates adverse media outputs into structured workflows and consolidated case management.
final_title: Connect UBO adverse media into audit-ready cases
final_description: Review how SFTY structures adverse media results, applies rules, preserves evidence and supports end-to-end AML compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
